text.title: filesystem-as-working-memory Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for filesystem-as-working-memory as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Summarizing the conversation at the first sign of pressure (50-60% utilization) instead of offloading the next tool result.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lossy compression applied while reversible space-savings are still available — the agent loses recoverable facts to save tokens it didn't need to save yet.

failure-mode.description: FS in tmpfs / per-process RAM, conversation in Postgres — process restart leaves the agent with file paths that 404.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Memory model splits across two persistence boundaries; agent's references decay independently of its own awareness of them.

failure-mode.description: Compaction routine fires whenever context utilization crosses ~50%, replacing the conversation with a summary while large tool outputs are still inline.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The lossless lever (offload to FS) was still available; the agent burned facts it could have kept.

failure-mode.description: Tool reads a file, returns the full content to the model, model emits a tool call to write the same content to a different file.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The blob crosses the model boundary twice for no reason — pay the input + output token cost on data the model never needed to "see".

failure-mode.description: Agent stores files in /tmp or in-memory dict, conversation in durable store; restart leaves dangling paths.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Two persistence boundaries — agent's mental model assumes the file still exists because the message that mentions it does.
